heading: Members of Army National Guard of United States and Air National Guard of United States: credit for service as members of National Guard
label: 12602 Members of Army National Guard of United States and Air National Guard of United States: credit for service as members of National Guard
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in sections 3686 and 8686 of this title, prior to repeal by .Pub. L. 103–337, § 1662(g)(2)
Effective DateSection effective , except as otherwise provided, see , set out as a note under .Dec. 1, 1994section 1691 of Pub. L. 103–337section 10001 of this title

children.text: military training, duty, or other service performed by a member of the Army National Guard of the United States in his status as a member of the Army National Guard for which he is entitled to pay from the United States shall be considered military training, duty, or other service, as the case may be, in Federal service as a Reserve of the Army;
full-time National Guard duty performed by a member of the Army National Guard of the United States shall be considered active duty in Federal service as a Reserve of the Army; and

inactive-duty training performed by a member of the Army National Guard of the United States in his status as a member of the Army National Guard, in accordance with regulations prescribed under or other express provision of law, shall be considered inactive-duty training in Federal service as a Reserve of the Army.section 502 of title 32

military training, duty, or other service performed by a member of the Air National Guard of the United States in his status as a member of the Air National Guard for which he is entitled to pay from the United States shall be considered military training, duty, or other service, as the case may be, in Federal service as a Reserve of the Air Force;
full-time National Guard duty performed by a member of the Air National Guard of the United States shall be considered active duty in Federal service as a Reserve of the Air Force; and

inactive-duty training performed by a member of the Air National Guard of the United States in his status as a member of the Air National Guard, in accordance with regulations prescribed under or other express provision of law, shall be considered inactive-duty training in Federal service as a Reserve of the Air Force.section 502 of title 32
